summary.en: Foundational figure in Western philosophy; his method focused on questioning.
Student of Socrates; founded the Academy; highly influential on Al-Farabi.
Known as 'The First Teacher'; his works on logic and metaphysics formed the core of the Islamic Golden Age translations.
His medical texts were the gold standard for Islamic physicians like Al-Razi and Ibn Sina.
His 'Almagest' provided the astronomical foundation for Islamic science.
Father of Geometry; his 'Elements' was fundamental to Islamic mathematical progress.
Greek mathematician and engineer whose works on spheres and cylinders were translated into Arabic.
